Prompt
U
Oracle Events
1) Search Slack for David Chen's message requesting the at-risk account status brief using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "at-risk" or "status brief" or "Emeka") or conversations_history (channel_id: "C002"). Find David's message (ts: 1777266600.000000) in #customer-engagement requesting a consolidated status brief on Mosaic Robotics, GreenStack Energy, and Sunbelt Venture Partners before his Monday leadership sync with Elena and Marcus.
2) Search emails for Tamara Byrd's escalation using search_emails (query: "Tamara" or "Mosaic" or "incident report"). Find email from tamara.byrd@mosaicrobotics.com (email_id: email_email_a7c3f19d4e02), CC'ing rafael.mendes@mosaicrobotics.com, replying to Emeka's incident report. Tamara demands: full $90,000 direct reimbursement (not waiting for Heartland recovery), permanent removal of $1,200 crating surcharge, replacement transport plan with specific conditions and replacement unit delivered by May 8. Overall deadline: April 30 for MoveOps to respond or Mosaic evaluates alternative vendors for remaining Q2/Q3 relocations.
3) Search emails for Andre Washington's stipend complaint using search_emails (query: "Andre Washington" or "stipend" or "reimbursement"). Find email from andre.washington@mosaicrobotics.com (email_id: email_email_b8d4e20a5f13) CC'ing tamara.byrd@mosaicrobotics.com. Andre complains about: $89 keyboard reimbursement held for 46 days, $1,200 standing desk auto-approved but exceeds $800 policy cap, $350/month coworking membership auto-approved then flagged as outside policy.
4) Search emails for Emeka's incident report context using search_emails (query: "incident report" or "prototype damage" or "Zara Okoye"). Find Emeka's sent incident report (email_id: mosaic_incident_report_final_001) detailing: $90K prototype damaged, MoveOps vendor liability insurance covers max $50K per item leaving a $40K gap, MoveOps pursuing recovery from Heartland for the uncovered amount, $1,200 crating surcharge placed on billing hold, credit memo CM-2026-0415 applied.
5) Search emails for prior Andre Washington stipend context using search_emails (query: "TechComfort" or "STIP-2026-0094" or "duplicate receipt" or "auto-categorizer"). Find: Dmitri's audit flagged Andre's $89 keyboard receipt as byte-for-byte duplicate of Kevin Tran's at Vectral, Marcus placed administrative hold on STIP-2026-0094 ($89, not yet disbursed), Emeka emailed Tamara on March 12 to verify the receipt, Emeka separately reported auto-categorizer false approvals ($1,200 standing desk exceeded $800 policy cap, $350/month coworking excluded from Mosaic policy).
6) Search emails for Derek Hollis's DOT compliance email using search_emails (query: "Derek" or "GreenStack" or "DOT" or "PHMSA"). Find email from derek.hollis@greenstackenergy.com (email_id: email_email_c9e5f31b6024) requesting: written confirmation MoveOps is shipper of record, Swift's DOT hazmat certification and placarding certificate, confirmation UN3481 packaging was MoveOps/carrier responsibility, PHMSA inquiry status (Case No. PHMSA-2026-04-1187) and whether GreenStack will be named. Also asks about Mei-Lin Zhao's Portland start date (May 5) and Joaquin Reyes rig transport.
7) Search emails for DOT/PHMSA inquiry status internally using search_emails (query: "PHMSA" or "DOT extension" or "Linda Castellano"). Find: DOT granted extension to June 5, 2026 (email from linda.castellano@dot.gov), Marcus forwarded extension to Chloe with instructions on documentation requirements (signed originals, training records per 49 CFR 172.704(d)), Marcus asked about Swift's signed placarding cert status.
8) Search emails for Raj Greenfield's pricing demand using search_emails (query: "Raj" or "Sunbelt" or "pricing" or "discount"). Find email from raj.greenfield@sunbeltvp.com (email_id: email_email_d0f6a42c7135) CC'ing victoria.chen-nakamura@sunbeltvp.com. Raj demands: 20% reduction on all relocation fees retroactive to April and going forward for Q2/Q3, threatens to pause all new requests if not accommodated, three portfolio companies considering MoveOps for upcoming relocations now on hold. Proposes call with Emeka, David, and Victoria.
9) Search emails for Sunbelt invoice swap history using search_emails (query: "INV-2026-0398" or "Palmetto" or "Sunbelt invoice"). Find: Hana sent correct $4,800 invoice (INV-2026-0398) but wrong PDF attached (Palmetto's $2,400 nonprofit invoice), Raj asked about the discrepancy, Marcus analyzed the dual problem (pricing exposure + nonprofit audit trail risk), David framed it as a repeat pricing-exposure event (similar to Terraform incident), Samira confirmed the engineering root cause (missing recipient-vs-attachment validation), Emeka sent Raj an explanation email, Victoria Chen-Nakamura then inquired about portfolio-wide pricing.
10) Look up contact details for email recipients using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Tamara Byrd"), contacts_search_contacts (query: "Derek Hollis"), contacts_search_contacts (query: "Raj Greenfield"), contacts_search_contacts (query: "Victoria Chen-Nakamura"), contacts_search_contacts (query: "David Chen"). Find tamara.byrd@mosaicrobotics.com, derek.hollis@greenstackenergy.com, raj.greenfield@sunbeltvp.com, victoria.chen-nakamura@sunbeltvp.com, david.chen@moveops.com.
11) Look up CRM company records for engagement logging using crm_search_companies (name: "Mosaic Robotics"), crm_search_companies (name: "GreenStack Energy"), crm_search_companies (name: "Sunbelt Venture Partners"). Find company IDs: company_mosaic, company_greenstack, company_sunbelt.
12) Post a consolidated status brief to David Chen in #customer-engagement using conversations_add_message (channel_id: "C002", payload: status update covering all three accounts). Content covers Mosaic (Tamara's April 30 deadline for $90K reimbursement demand, Andre's stipend issues), GreenStack (Derek's DOT compliance concerns, Mei-Lin/Joaquin relocation status), Sunbelt (Raj's 20% discount demand, Victoria's involvement). Includes honest assessment of client temperature, outstanding commitments, what can be delivered this week, and what needs escalation (reimbursement approval from Marcus/Elena, pricing authority from David).
13) Send email response to Tamara Byrd using reply_to_email (email_id: "email_email_a7c3f19d4e02", sender: "emeka.diallo@moveops.com") or send_email (sender: "emeka.diallo@moveops.com", recipients: ["tamara.byrd@mosaicrobotics.com"]). CC rafael.mendes@mosaicrobotics.com. Content addresses her demands point by point: does NOT commit to the full $90,000 reimbursement (needs Marcus and Elena approval), acknowledges the $1,200 crating surcharge removal request, acknowledges the replacement transport conditions and May 8 delivery timeline, provides a concrete timeline for when the reimbursement decision will be communicated.
14) Send email response to Derek Hollis using reply_to_email (email_id: "email_email_c9e5f31b6024", sender: "emeka.diallo@moveops.com") or send_email (sender: "emeka.diallo@moveops.com", recipients: ["derek.hollis@greenstackenergy.com"]). Content addresses his documentation requests: confirms what MoveOps can provide, flags that shipper-of-record confirmation and PHMSA inquiry status need verification with Marcus Thorne and Chloe Vance, provides update on Mei-Lin Zhao's equipment delivery timeline (May 5 start date) and Joaquin Reyes rig transport status, commits to a follow-up timeline.
15) Send email response to Raj Greenfield using reply_to_email (email_id: "email_email_d0f6a42c7135", sender: "emeka.diallo@moveops.com") or send_email (sender: "emeka.diallo@moveops.com", recipients: ["raj.greenfield@sunbeltvp.com"]). CC victoria.chen-nakamura@sunbeltvp.com. Content acknowledges Raj's concerns about the pricing differential, proposes a call this week with Raj, David Chen, and Victoria Chen-Nakamura to discuss pricing, does NOT commit to or promise any specific discount percentage. Tone is warm and relationship-focused.
16) Create CRM engagement note for Mosaic Robotics using crm_create_engagement (engagement_type: "NOTE", company_ids: [company_mosaic], body: summary of today's escalation covering Tamara's rejection of remediation proposal, $90K reimbursement demand, April 30 deadline, Andre Washington's stipend complaints).
17) Create CRM engagement note for GreenStack Energy using crm_create_engagement (engagement_type: "NOTE", company_ids: [company_greenstack], body: summary of today's escalation covering Derek's DOT compliance concerns, legal team involvement, Mei-Lin and Joaquin relocation updates requested).
18) Create CRM engagement note for Sunbelt Venture Partners using crm_create_engagement (engagement_type: "NOTE", company_ids: [company_sunbelt], body: summary of today's escalation covering Raj's 20% discount demand, Victoria Chen-Nakamura involvement, portfolio referral leverage, proposed pricing call).
19) Schedule a 30-minute prep sync with David Chen using calendar_add_calendar_event (title: "Prep sync with David" or similar, start_datetime: "2026-04-27T09:00:00-07:00" for Monday April 27 at 9 AM Pacific, end_datetime: "2026-04-27T09:30:00-07:00", description: prep for leadership meeting on at-risk accounts).

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

✓Run #2A calendar_add_calendar_event call was made with start_datetime '2026-04-27 09:00:00' and end_datetime '2026-04-27 09:30:00', making it a 30-minute event on Monday April 27, 2026. The tool returned a success event_id.

✗Run #4The calendar event was scheduled with start_datetime '2026-04-27 12:00:00' (no timezone specified). The agent described this as '12:00 PM ET' (equivalent to 9 AM Pacific), but the tool parameter does not specify timezone as Pacific (-07:00). The criterion requires start_datetime at '09:00 Pacific (-07:00)'. The stored datetime is 12:00, not 09:00, and without an explicit timezone the call does not clearly match the required 9:00 AM Pacific specification.
✓Run #5calendar_add_calendar_event was called with start_datetime '2026-04-27 09:00:00' and end_datetime '2026-04-27 09:30:00' (30 minutes). April 27, 2026 is the Monday following the universe date of April 26 (Sunday). The tool returned a valid event_id confirming success.
✓Run #6calendar_add_calendar_event was called with start_datetime '2026-04-27 09:00:00' and end_datetime '2026-04-27 09:30:00', which is Monday April 27 at 9:00 AM for 30 minutes. Tool returned success with event_id.
